name: Credit Saint by Liberty Tax
description_short: Credit Saint is a credit repair company that disputes inaccurate negative items on credit reports. Since 2007, they've served 250,000+ customers with a 4.8-star rating across major review platforms.
description_long:
Credit Saint was founded in 2007 and has built a reputation as a credit repair service focused on disputing inaccurate or unfair negative items appearing on consumer credit reports. The company claims to have helped over 250,000 Americans address credit problems and maintains 19 years of operational history in the credit repair industry.

The company offers three tiered service plans—Credit Polish ($79.99/month), Credit Remodel ($109.99/month), and Clean Slate ($139.99/month)—each with an initial work fee. Their process involves three steps: (1) Setting up a secure online account and obtaining a free credit report, (2) Having a credit specialist review the report and send disputes to creditors and credit bureaus, and (3) Optimizing the credit profile with advice on leveraging positive history while disputes are pending. They target disputes related to collections, late payments, foreclosure, charge-offs, bankruptcy, judgments, repossessions, and liens.

Credit Saint differentiates itself through high review volume (11,000+ five-star reviews) and ranking claims on major financial sites. They emphasize a 90-day money-back guarantee and use of a secure online portal for client communication. The company highlights dedicated credit specialists and a focus on educating clients throughout the process.

However, consumers should note that Credit Saint cannot guarantee results or credit score increases, as stated in their own terms. Like all credit repair services, their value depends on whether clients actually have inaccurate items on their reports; disputing accurate negative information is ineffective. The monthly fees accumulate over time, making the total cost of repair potentially significant depending on how long disputes take to resolve.
pros: Established 19-year track record since 2007 with 250,000+ customers served
High review volume with 4.8-star rating and 11,000+ five-star reviews across Google, ConsumerAffairs, and Money.com
Three pricing tiers ($79.99–$139.99/month) allowing customers to match service level to their needs
Secure online portal for confidential information sharing and account management
Free credit report review without impacting credit score
Covers multiple dispute categories including collections, late payments, foreclosure, charge-offs, bankruptcy, judgments, repossessions, and liens
cons: Monthly subscription fees accumulate over time—no guarantee how long disputes take to resolve, potentially costing $800+ annually or more
Cannot promise results or credit score increases per their own disclaimer; only effective if items on report are actually inaccurate
Competing against consumers' right to dispute for free directly with credit bureaus under federal law
Initial work fees ($99–$195) add upfront cost on top of monthly subscription
No transparency on website regarding average dispute resolution timelines or success rates for specific dispute types
best_for: Consumers with multiple inaccurate negative items (collections, late payments, foreclosures) who prefer outsourcing the dispute process
Individuals with limited time or knowledge to navigate credit bureau dispute procedures independently
People with complex credit histories involving judgments, liens, or bankruptcy seeking professional guidance through optimization
Consumers who value personalized credit specialist review over DIY or fully automated dispute services
diagnosis: Credit Saint is best for consumers with verifiably inaccurate negative items on their credit reports who prefer professional dispute handling over self-service options. The main caveat is that their paid service competes with consumers' free right to dispute directly with credit bureaus, and results depend entirely on whether reported items are actually inaccurate—no service can remove accurate negative information.
services: Credit report review and analysis by credit specialists
Disputing inaccurate or unfair negative items with creditors and credit bureaus
Secure online account portal for document sharing and communication
Free credit report pull without credit score impact
Credit optimization advice to leverage positive payment history
